concepts.title: What is 0G? The Decentralized AI Operating System
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- 0G is the world's first decentralized AI operating system (deAIOS)
- It provides infrastructure — storage, compute, data availability — so developers don't build from scratch
- Purpose-built for AI workloads: high throughput, low cost, trustless guarantees

0G Labs describes 0G as a 'decentralized AI operating system' — and the analogy is intentional. Just as an operating system (Windows, Linux, macOS) provides a foundation that applications build on top of, 0G provides foundational AI infrastructure that any developer or application can use without building their own data centers.

Think about what it takes to build an AI application today:
- You need a place to store training datasets and model weights (terabytes of data)
- You need GPUs to train and run inference
- You need a way to verify that computations happened correctly
- You need a blockchain for provenance and payments

All of this typically comes from centralized cloud providers: AWS for storage, OpenAI or Bedrock for inference, Ethereum for on-chain logic. Each provider charges a markup for their infrastructure.

0G replaces this stack with a single, unified, decentralized platform:
- **Cheaper**: 95% less than AWS for storage, 90% less than OpenAI for inference
- **Permissionless**: No API keys to request, no accounts to approve
- **Trustless**: Cryptographic proofs replace trust in any single company
- **Composable**: The four services work together natively

For AI applications specifically, 0G removes the infrastructure bottleneck. Developers can focus on building instead of managing cloud services.

concepts.title: The Four Services: Chain, Storage, Compute, DA
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- 0G Chain: EVM-compatible L1 blockchain, 11,000 TPS, smart contracts
- 0G Storage: Decentralized file storage, 95% cheaper than AWS S3
- 0G Compute: Decentralized GPU marketplace, 90% cheaper than OpenAI
- 0G DA: Data availability layer, 50 Gbps throughput for rollups

0G has four distinct services, each solving a specific infrastructure problem for AI applications:

**0G Chain — The Programmable Layer**
This is an EVM-compatible Layer 1 blockchain. If you know Ethereum, you already know 0G Chain — the same Solidity contracts, MetaMask wallets, and ethers.js code work without modification. It processes 11,000 transactions per second (vs Ethereum's ~15 TPS) with sub-second finality. This is where your smart contracts live, where payments settle, and where data provenance is recorded.

**0G Storage — The File System**
Decentralized storage for any file type: datasets, model weights, documents, images. Files are content-addressed (identified by their Merkle root hash) and replicated across hundreds of nodes. No single company holds your data. Price: ~5% of AWS S3. Retrieval speed: 200 MBPS. This is where your AI's data lives.

**0G Compute — The Execution Engine**
A marketplace of GPU providers running AI inference and fine-tuning. The API is OpenAI-compatible — existing apps can switch with two lines of code. Providers compete on price, driving costs 90% below centralized alternatives. This is where your AI thinking happens.

**0G DA — The Data Availability Backbone**
High-throughput data availability for rollups (Layer 2 blockchains). At 50 Gbps, it's orders of magnitude above Ethereum's DA capacity. If you're building a rollup or L2, this is where your transaction data goes for verification without full on-chain storage.

For most developers getting started, you'll interact with Chain, Storage, and Compute. DA becomes relevant when you're building at L2 scale.

concepts.title: Why Decentralized AI Infrastructure Matters
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- Centralized AI creates single points of failure and censorship
- Decentralization enables permissionless access and verifiable computation
- Open competition between providers drives costs down dramatically

Why not just use AWS or OpenAI? This is a fair question, and understanding the answer helps you use 0G more effectively.

**The centralization problem:**

When you use AWS S3, you're trusting Amazon to:
- Keep your data available
- Not change pricing unexpectedly
- Not delete or censor your data
- Not be hacked or go down

When you use OpenAI's API, you're trusting OpenAI to:
- Keep running their service
- Not rate-limit or ban your application
- Provide accurate AI responses
- Not change pricing or terms

For many applications, this trust is acceptable. But for AI applications that need to operate at scale, across geographies, or in adversarial environments, centralization is a liability.

**The economics of decentralization:**

Centralized providers charge market rates because there's limited competition at scale. AWS S3 pricing includes Amazon's infrastructure costs plus a significant markup for their support, reliability, and profit margin.

0G flips this: hundreds of storage miners and GPU operators compete directly for your work. The Flow smart contract and Compute Ledger act as trustless marketplace coordinators. Competition drives prices to hardware costs — that's why 0G achieves 95% cost reduction for storage and 90% for compute.

**The verifiability advantage:**

0G uses cryptographic proofs to verify that:
- Your file is actually stored (Proof of Random Access)
- The AI inference was actually performed correctly (ZK proofs)
- Data is available for rollup verification (KZG commitments)

You don't have to trust anyone — the math proves it.

concepts.title: The 0G Ecosystem: Networks, Tokens, and Resources
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- Two networks: Testnet (Galileo, chainId 16602) and Mainnet (Aristotle, chainId 16661)
- Free testnet tokens at https://faucet.0g.ai for development
- Explorers at chainscan-galileo.0g.ai (testnet) and chainscan.0g.ai (mainnet)

Before building on 0G, it helps to understand the ecosystem structure.

**Two Networks:**

| Network | Name | Chain ID | RPC |
|---------|------|----------|-----|
| Testnet | Galileo | 16602 | https://evmrpc-testnet.0g.ai |
| Mainnet | Aristotle | 16661 | https://evmrpc.0g.ai |

Always develop and test on the Testnet first. Testnet tokens are free and have no real value — perfect for experimenting. Mainnet tokens have real value and are used for production applications.

**Getting Testnet Tokens:**
1. Go to https://faucet.0g.ai
2. Connect your MetaMask wallet or paste your address
3. Receive 0.1 0G per day (free)

0G tokens are used for gas fees on 0G Chain. For storage and compute, you'll fund those services separately.

**Key Resources:**
- **Documentation**: https://docs.0g.ai
- **Builder Hub**: https://build.0g.ai
- **Chain Explorer (Testnet)**: https://chainscan-galileo.0g.ai
- **Chain Explorer (Mainnet)**: https://chainscan.0g.ai
- **Storage Explorer**: https://storagescan.0g.ai
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/0gfoundation

**The 0G Token (0G):**
The native token is used for gas fees on 0G Chain. For Storage, you pay in 0G tokens to miners via the Flow contract. For Compute, you deposit 0G tokens into an escrow that pays GPU providers. Everything is denominated in the same token, simplifying multi-service applications.

quiz.question: Where can you get free 0G tokens to test your application?
options: You must purchase them on a crypto exchange
https://faucet.0g.ai — provides 0.1 0G per day for free
They are automatically airdropped to new wallets
